But I can give you an insight into the keybindings I use on a daily basis and how I personally make sense of them. Hopefully this helps you prioritize which keybindings to learn first and how to remember them. The keybindings listed here work in normal and visual mode, but not in insert mode, where your keystrokes will obviously be interpreted as text input.

For navigating in a file, I use the following keybindings:

  • G: Go to the end of the file
  • gg: Go to the beginning of the file
  • $: Go to the end of the line
  • 0: Go to the beginning of the line
  • e: Move to the end of the current word
  • b: Move to the beginning of the current word

And I do have to confess I am one of the few Vim users who actually uses the arrow keys to move around (I know, I know), instead of h, j, k, and l. I have to reasons for that: First of all, I just couldn't get used to them. Second of all, I often find myself wanting to navigate around in insert mode, and the arrow keys are just more convenient in that situation than switching modes.

For editing text, I use the following keybindings:

  • i: Enter insert mode
  • a: Enter insert mode after the current cursor position (one character further right than with i)
  • A: Enter insert mode at the end of the line
  • u: Undo the last change
  • Ctrl-r: Redo the last change
  • dw: Delete the current word
  • dd: Delete the current line
  • D: Delete from the current cursor position to the end of the line

For selecting text and copy-pasta magic, I use the following keybindings:

  • v: Enter visual mode
  • V: Enter visual line mode (visually select whole line)
  • y: Yank (copy) the selected text (in visual mode)
  • d: Delete the selected text (in visual mode)
  • c: Change the selected text (in visual mode, delete and enter insert mode)
  • p: Paste the yanked text after the cursor

With this set of keybindings (that barely scratches the surface of what's possible) you can actually accomplish a lot of things, especially when combining them. For example, for navigating to the end of the file and going into insert mode at the end of the last line, you can simply use GA. There are probably more efficient ways to get certain things done, but this "minimalistic" set of keybindings works well for me and most importantly, I can remember them. This is just the start and there is a lot more to learn, but that takes time and practice. I will try and add more keybindings to my repertoire as I go along. It's a fun journey.

And that's it for this blog post. Let's talk about the language server and how you can tailor it to your liking next time - arguably the most important and coolest part of a modern development environment.

entry.title: AI@FRE (PART 4) - Guardrail Enterprise AI Adoption – AI Architecture Guardrails
entry.content:
### AI@FRE Blog Series:

AI@FRE (Part 1) - Enabling Enterprise AI Adoption through the BTP FRE Engagement Model

AI@FRE (Part 2) - Accelerating Business AI Transformation with the FRE Value Framework

AI@FRE (Part 3) – Guardrails for Enterprise AI Adoption

AI@FRE (PART 4) - Guardrail Enterprise AI Adoption – AI Architecture Guardrails 

###

Scaling enterprise AI is no longer optional; it's essential for organizations looking to remain competitive. However, achieving this requires robust architecture and tool standardization to enable scalable, secure, and efficient AI deployment. Standardizing key areas in an organization, from security to automation, digital experiences, core, data, and AI architecture, sets a strong foundation for streamlined processes, enhanced agility, and a simplified landscape. 

The Importance of Standardizing Architecture and Tools for AI at Scale 

Building a scalable AI infrastructure hinges on standardizing architecture and tools, which enables enterprises to measure key outcomes like: 

  • Business Agility & Flexibility: Achieve XX-month reduction in time-to-market for new AI initiatives. 
  • Execution Speed: Drive XX-day reduction in project execution. 
  • Simplified Landscape: Foster streamlined architecture that minimizes redundancies and supports easy upgrades and scalability. 

Each area of standardization brings unique benefits and helps ensure consistent integration across SAP solutions, as we’ll explore below. 

 

  1. Standardize Security Architecture 

With AI adoption, ensuring security at every layer of the architecture is critical. A standardized security architecture enables cohesive, scalable, and adaptive access management. 

  • User Access Management (IPS): Enforces access control, minimizing security risks by managing permissions and restricting access to sensitive data. 
  • Single Sign-On (IAS): Unifies login experiences across SAP solutions, enabling employees to access tools seamlessly. 
  • Automated, Adaptive Access Management: Automates access protocols to ensure compliance, adaptive access control, and lifecycle management. 
  • Event-Driven Identity Lifecycle Management: Automates identity management processes in response to organizational events, streamlining updates to user access and permissions. 

Business Benefits: 

  • Unified Access: Simplifies security management by centralizing access across SAP environments. 
  • Secure Remote Work: Provides reliable access for remote employees without compromising security. 
  • Enhanced Employee Experience: Eliminates repetitive logins, allowing employees to focus on tasks. 
  • Productivity and Collaboration: Reduces friction in accessing necessary tools, fostering productivity. 
  • Digital Transformation: Lays the security groundwork for digital transformation efforts across the organization. 

 

Enabling of single-sign-on and identity provisioning throughout your application landscape 

 

  1. Standardize Enterprise Automation 

Automating workflows and processes is essential for scaling AI-driven enterprise operations. Standardized automation supports consistent, efficient, and error-free processes. De Agostini Publishing S.p.A leveraged SAP BTP to automate vendor invoice management, enhancing accuracy, efficiency, and data reliability across new markets. Another example is from Colsubsidio’s SAP BTP implementation, which enables them efficient, independent analytics for strategic, data-driven decision-making. 

  • Automate Visually: Utilize intuitive, drag-and-drop interfaces and AI to simplify automation. 
  • Integrate Seamlessly: Speed up development with pre-built workflows, bots, and connectors for both SAP and non-SAP systems. 
  • Collaborate Securely: Empower cross-functional teams with secure, agile development tools. 

Automation Examples: 

  • Generative AI for Interactive Modeling: Supports modeling through natural language inputs with proactive validation. 
  • Document Information Extraction: Automates data capture from structured documents like invoices. 
  • Action Process AI (SAP Signavio): Combines large process models with SAP expertise to improve accuracy and effectiveness. 

Key Benefits: 

  • Reduced manual effort and error rates. 
  • Increased speed and quality of automation. 
  • Empowered cross-functional teams with collaboration capabilities. 

 

Process AI at SAP Signavio 

 

  1. Standardize Digital Experience Architecture for Conversational Experience 

To create a seamless digital experience, the architecture must support modularity and scalability, allowing for a unified user interface across devices and applications. 

  • Conversational UX: Enable conversational interfaces across desktop and mobile, enhancing the user experience with intuitive, end-to-end interactions. 
  • SAP Start for Cloud Solutions: A modular homepage offers a single point of entry with pre-configured access to cloud solutions like SAP SuccessFactors and S/4HANA Cloud. 
  • Joule in SAP Applications: SAP’s digital assistant, Joule, is embedded in applications to enhance user experience, starting with SAP Start and SAP SuccessFactors. 

AI-powered Talent Intelligence: 

  • Talent Optimization: Use AI to generate job descriptions and interview questions tailored to organizational needs, streamlining HR processes. 
  • Skills-Based Matching: Empower skills-based organizations with talent intelligence to identify and promote internal talent efficiently. 

Standardize UX Architecture – The SAP UX Story 

 

  1. Standardize Core - Building a Lean Core 

A lean core structure is essential to keep AI-enabled systems agile and cloud-compliant, enhancing scalability and reducing potential disruptions. 

  • Avoid Custom Extensions: Minimize customizations to ensure compatibility with SAP’s upgrade cycles. 
  • Separate Extensions with APIs: Ensure extensions do not interfere with upgrades by leveraging APIs. 
  • Cloud-Compliant Extensions: Develop with cloud compatibility in mind, aligning with a three-tier architectural model. 

Business Impact: 

  • Smooth upgrades, minimal disruptions. 
  • Scalable, cloud-ready architecture. 
  • Enhanced reliability in deployments. 

 

  1. Standardize Data Architecture 

Data architecture standardization is vital for ensuring seamless data sharing, comprehensive analytics, and powerful AI model training across the enterprise. 

  • Business Data Fabric: An architecture that delivers contextually rich data to every user, preserving business context and logic. 
  • Intelligent Data Applications: Enable developers to create apps that are AI-driven, context-aware, and seamlessly connected to core business data. 
  • Extended Planning and Analysis: Unify planning functions for finance, HR, and operations within a single, data-rich ecosystem. 
  • Data Sharing & Products: Facilitate data-sharing with data products that provide both abstraction and usability layers without introducing new artifacts. 

Example Use Case: 

  • Business Decision Simulator: Simulate complex scenarios to predict potential disruptions and make data-informed adjustments. 

Core Benefits: 

  • Holistic data access across departments. 
  • Stronger alignment between operational and strategic planning. 
  • Enhanced data-driven decision-making. 

 

  1. Standardize AI Architecture 

The AI architecture must support diverse AI capabilities, from natural language processing to generative AI, ensuring seamless integration and adaptability across SAP applications. 

  • Joule in All Apps: SAP’s digital assistant Joule, embedded in SAP applications, offers users insights and support while automating routine tasks. It allows users to directly access services like SAP SuccessFactors and S/4HANA Cloud. 
  • Generative AI Hub in SAP AI Core: The GenAI Hub provides a central location for generative AI models, enabling SAP applications to draw upon AI capabilities like text generation, summarization, and process modeling in real time. 
  • SAP-Hosted Foundation Models: By hosting foundational AI models, SAP AI Core offers scalability and reliability, reducing dependency on external AI solutions and ensuring secure data handling. 

Benefits: 

  • Scalability: Supports consistent deployment across all SAP applications, reducing the need for fragmented, custom AI solutions. 
  • Data Security: Centralizes model hosting within SAP’s secure environment. 
  • Enhanced Process Intelligence: Infuses advanced process and data understanding across SAP systems, allowing companies to optimize AI-driven workflows effectively. 

Conclusion 

Standardizing architecture and tools across these six key areas creates a solid foundation for scalable, secure, and efficient AI adoption in enterprise settings. This standardization empowers organizations to move faster, adapt more readily to changes, and achieve a simplified and agile landscape that supports continued innovation and growth. 

 

Curious on a structured customer engagement model to set up standardized Cloud Architecture for Business AI Adoption? Connect with our Global BTP Customer Engagement Team at BTPFRE@sap.com.

entry.title: What's New in SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition 2411?
entry.content:
SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition is a software-as-a-service (Saas) solution on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) that is used to govern core attributes of business partner data (more domains planned). It is perfectly suited for organizations kick-starting a master data management initiative at low entry barriers. For details, see also the SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition overview and  deep-dive presentations.

What's New?

Continuing the blog series about innovations in SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition, I'd like to provide you with details on the latest innovations with SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition 2411. 

General Remark

Generally, a new version of several existing apps is available to provide new functionalities, for example, simulation of validation rules and additional process attributes. You can recognize the previous versions that have now been deprecated by the Deprecated label on the tiles. Note that a user can only use either all deprecated or all new versions when working with SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition. We recommend using the new version of all apps as soon as possible, as the deprecated versions will be removed in the next release.

Updates in Data Quality Management

Data quality management comes with new apps to support derivation scenarios, validation rules and analytics, and a new version of the validation rules app. 

New and enhanced apps in data quality management

In detail, the increased scope in data quality management for business partner data, enables master data specialists to:

  • Define derivation scenarios
    • With the new Define Derivation Scenarios for Business Partners app, you can create and edit derivation scenarios for your business partner data. You use derivation scenarios to deduce master data based on rules and a defined scope to ensure data quality.
  • Import/export of derivation scenarios
    • With the new Import Derivation Scenarios app, you can import derivation scenarios and their rules, which you previously downloaded using the Export Validation Rules app. Validation rules, their business details and their BRFplus implementation can be imported.

Importing derivation scenarios - log details

  • Import/export of validation rules
    • With the new Import Validation Rules app, you can import validation rules, which you previously downloaded using the Export Validation Rules app. Validation rules, their business details and their BRFplus implementation can be imported.
    • With the new Export Validation Rules app, you can download validation rules, which are managed in the Validation Rules for Business Partners app, to then import them into another system. Validation rule exports contain validation rules, their business details and their BRFplus implementation.

Exporting validation rules

  • The new version of the Validation Rules for Business Partners app offers the following new features:
    • Copy validation rules
    • Simulate validation rules to check if they work as intended and to get a preview of the rule results
    • Check if the rule is assigned to any dimensions, which is done in the Configure Data Quality Scores for Business Partners app descibed in the following:
    • The new Configure Data Quality Scores app allows key users to define their data quality reporting structure by using Categories and Dimensions. The aggregation of rules scores to data quality dimensions and categories is as follows:
Categories
  • Multiple categories supported
  • Score: Average of dimension scores 
  • Thresholds: Target, Warning, Critical 
Dimensions
  • Multiple dimensions supported 
  • Score: weighted average of rule scores 
Rules
  • Assigned to dimension with Impact
  • Multiple assignments possible, but only once per category

 Improvements in Business Partner Hierarchy Management

New features in Track Changes – Business Partner Hierarchies and Track Changes – Business Partner Hierarchy Nodes apps

  • A value help was added to the Business Partner Hierarchy and Business Partner Hierarchy Node fields. In the Track Changes - Business Partner Hierarchy Nodes app, you can search for a hierarchy ID or hierarchy description to find the associated nodes.

New features in new Manage Business Partner Hierarchies app

  • The following new features are available in the new version of the Manage Business Partner Hierarchies app:

    • The node ID is now generated automatically if you leave the field empty. The generated node ID is based on the hierarchy ID and a counter.

    • The system can generate node descriptions automatically. To generate one, add a business partner to a node and choose Accept. The first and second word of the business partner will be used as node description.
    • The search filters Business Partner and Hierarchy Node ID are now available. These filters can help you to find the associated hierarchy.
    • You can now edit a hierarchy that is in a process in the Process Information tab. In this tab, you can change the Process Description, Process Template, Priority, Due Date, and Reason.
    • When you make changes to your hierarchy, you can now see the Node Status column in the app. Edited nodes are displayed as Changed or Inserted. Deleted nodes disappear from the hierarchy.

Additional Improvements in Business Partner Processing

Additional innovations include:

  • With the new My Governance Processes - Business Partner app you can display business partner processes that have been started by you. The system displays the list of processes as well as the number of business partners, BP relationships and hierarchies.

My Governance Processes app showing all running processes initiated by oneself

  • With a new feature in the Maintain process attributes for Manage Business Partners and Manage Business Processes apps you can now maintain special attributes such as priority, due date or reason to categorize and analyze master data processes when you create or change a business partner. This information can be changed as long as the process is not activated or cancelled.

Categorization of master data creation process by priority, reason, and due date

For an overview of the innovations specified above, see also the What's New Viewer - SAP Master Data Governance, Cloud Edition 2411

Continued innovations in SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition help organizations in managing their core business partner data master for their entire system landscape. 

To stay on track, simply follow the SAP Community Topic Page for SAP Master Data Governance, read the MDG-related blogs, and join the  Q&A forum.

Best,

Markus

entry.title: Enabling Access to On-Premise S/4HANA Applications in SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition: Step-by
entry.content:
Subscriptions

  • SAP Business Technology Platform
  • SAP Cloud Connector
  • SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition
  • S/4 HANA On-Premises System Setup

Pre-requisite Setup

  • Setup of SAP Business Technology Platform Account
  • Setup of SAP Cloud Connector & Trust to the SAP S/4HANA System
  • Setup of S/4HANA on-premises System
  • Setup SAP Identity and Authentication Service (optional)

Pre-requisite Checks

  1. In your BTP subaccount you are subscribed to the following subscriptions
  • Cloud Identity Services

 

  • SAP Business Application Studio (Automatically Added)

 

  • SAP Build Work Zone, Standard edition

SAP BTP Subaccount --> Instances and Subscriptions --> create

 

  • Environment – SAP Cloud Foundry Instance

 

  1. Configure Cloud Connector with S/4 HANA On-Premise System with the following details –

For the installation of the Cloud Connector please follow the steps in the SAP Help document SAP Help

You can skip installing the Cloud Connector if you’re using a demo backend system from SAP Cloud Appliance Library (CAL). These demo systems come with a pre-installed Cloud Connector that’s ready to use. To access it, go to the Welcome page in your CAL system, where you’ll find the relevant links. The Cloud Connector typically runs at http://<IP of your SAP CAL backend>:8443/, where you can directly connect to it.

 

  1. Check your BTP Connectivity with Cloud Connector:- login to your BTP Subaccount and go to Destinations, and select  Create Destination with the following details :

 

 

Field

Value

Name

Name of the Destination

Type

HTTP

URL

http://Virtual Host Port No.(443XX)

Proxy Type

OnPremise

Authentication

BasicAuthentication

User

Support User Created in On-Premise System

 

 

           

 

  1. Once the Connection is successful, test the mapping created in Cloud Connector, it should be reachable.

 

 

Execution Steps

 

Create a Site in SAP Build Work Zone

In SAP Build Work Zone, a "site" refers to a digital workspace or portal where users can access applications, content, and resources. It is the central hub for delivering a personalized and unified experience to employees, partners, or customers. Sites in SAP Build Work Zone are highly customizable and can be designed to meet specific user needs and business requirements.

  1. Login to SAP BTP Subaccount and go to instances and subscriptions, choose SAP Build Work Zone and Go to Application

 

 

In a new window, you will log in to SAP Build Work Zone Site.

Here Select Create Site.

Keep the default settings for the site same and your site is created.

Note – Make sure you have the necessary authorizations assigned to your user ID in SAP BTP. The following roles are mandatory – Launchpad_Admin, Launchpad_External_User

In SAP BTP Subaccount to Security à Usersà select Assign User Collection

 

 

Assign these roles to your User ID.

Create Destinations in SAP BTP

To display different types of app interfaces in SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition (like on-premise SAP S/4HANA apps and cloud-based apps), you first need to set up a way for Work Zone to access them. This is done by creating HTTP connections, called as "runtime destination, design time destination" in the SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform) cockpit. This connection acts as a bridge, allowing Work Zone to securely access and display apps from both your on-premise systems and the cloud.

Creating Design-time Destination

The design-time destination defines the location from which to fetch the content that is exposed by the content provider in SAP Build Work Zone.

  1. In the SAP BTP cockpit, log on to your subaccount as administrator.
  2. Go to Connectivity --> Destinations.
  3. To configure the design-time destination, choose New Destination and enter the following information:

 

Field

Value

Name

Add the dt suffix to the name

Type

HTTP

URL

https://<VIRTUAL_HOST>:<VIRTUAL_PORT>/sap/bc/ui2/cdm3/entities

Proxy Type

OnPremise

Authentication

BasicAuthentication

User

To connect to the SAP S/4HANA system, use a technical user on the destination with permission to read the CDM, for example, the technical user with the SAP_FLP_EXP_USER role, such as <SAP S/4HANA user ID> Creating System User in SAP

 

 

By choosing Additional Properties  New Property -->  sap-client: <client number>

Check the Connection for the Destination.

 

Creating Run-time Destination

The runtime destination defines the location from which to consume the content required to run the federated apps in runtime.

  1. To configure the runtime destination, choose New Destination and enter the following information:

Field

Value

Name

Add the rt suffix to the name

Type

HTTP

URL

https://<VIRTUAL_HOST>:<VIRTUAL_PORT>/sap/bc/ui2/cdm3/entities

Proxy Type

OnPremise

Authentication

BasicAuthentication

User

To connect to the SAP S/4HANA system, use a technical user on the destination with permission to read the CDM, for example, the technical user with the SAP_FLP_EXP_USER role, such as <SAP S/4HANA user ID> Creating System User in SAP

 

Add Following Additional Properties to complete configuration of runtime destination.

Field

Value

HTML5.DynamicDestination

true

sap-client

Client No

sap-platform

ABAP

sap-sysid

On-Premise System ID

sap-service

32XX (XX- virtual Port)

And Check the Connection for this destination it should be successful.

 

Note - The destination name must contain only lowercase letters and numbers. Do not use the underscore (_) character.

Expose Authorization from On-Premise System

This step makes your on-premise SAP roles accessible in SAP Build Work Zone, allowing end users to access integrated Fiori applications directly within the Work Zone site.

  1. Login to SAP On-Premise system and go to T-code - /UI2/CDM3_EXP_SCOPE

Add the relevant SAP Roles you want to expose to SAP Build Work Zone.

  1. Click on Expose Content

Make sure the execution is without any errors.

Configure Site in Work Zone

  1. Log in to your SAP Build Work Zone Instance, navigate to Content Channel --> New --> Content Provider

 

  1. Add the Following Details and Save.

Note – Please specify your correct runtime and design time destinations created for build work zone.

  1.  Update the content, here the content provider should get updated without any errors.

In case you receive any errors in connection please verify your destination names , for mapping errors check connectivity with the cloud connector.

Once the content is updated you will have your roles exposed from on premise available in work zone site.

  1. Update the Content From Content Provider

Select your content provider

Add your exposed roles

Roles are now visible in content manager

  1. Go to site Directory --> Settings --> Edit

The exposed roles will be available,

Assign them to the site and Save.

  1. Add the roles in your BTP subaccount

Login to your BTP Subaccount with administrator user and go to security --> Users --> Assign Role Collections

Assign the exposed SAP roles to the user

  1. Now in your SAP Build Work Zone Site the apps are visible

entry.title: SAP Sustainability Control Tower: Q3-Q4 2024 Updates & Highlights
entry.content:
Co-Authors: @bilgeakdur @amandamasuku 

Welcome to the future of ESG Reporting and Management with SAP Sustainability Control Tower! This blog post will explore the latest developments in the SAP Sustainability Control Tower and emphasize its crucial role in advancing your Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) agenda.

Positioned at the forefront of sustainability solutions, SAP Sustainability Control Tower is an ERP-centric, cloud-based platform designed to seamlessly integrate with SAP S/4HANA Cloud and other SAP solutions, including SAP Datasphere, SAP EHS Management, SAP Sustainability Footprint Management and SAP Analytics Cloud. These integration capabilities ensure sustainability is embedded into core business processes, providing automated, auditable, and compliant reporting for ESG data. 

Leveraging AI-driven features, the platform also automates the generation of comprehensive ESG reports and enables businesses to make informed decisions. 

With a strategic focus on expanding regulatory coverage and facilitating integration across solutions, SAP Sustainability Control Tower empowers organizations to achieve their sustainability goals while gaining a competitive advantage in the market.

The following features play a central role in the reporting capabilities solution. Initially, the ESG Data Foundation within the SAP Sustainability Control Tower offers a predefined data model designed to comply with regulatory standards by using pre-built and custom-built sustainability metrics. The solution is transitioning from supporting voluntary reports such as World Economic Forum (WEF) metrics to adopting ESRS and EU Taxonomy, which can also support customers who are starting to address SEC regulations and ISSB standards. Concurrently, this data foundation can also be used for Corporate Carbon Footprint (CCF) calculation and GHG reporting. 

Stay tuned as we explore the key features and benefits of the SAP Sustainability Control Tower:

  • Carbon Management Features of SAP SCT
  • Generate ESG Reports with AI
  • Disclosure Management Strategy
  • Data Foundation and Extensibility
  • Metric Management
  • Auditability
  • SAP Continuous Influence Program
  • Planned Feature Updates
  • Further Assets

 

Carbon Management Features of SAP SCT

As of June 2024, SCT's corporate carbon management capabilities enable you to accurately calculate both direct and indirect emissions, ensuring compliance and aiding in decarbonization efforts across your operations and supply chains. You can efficiently manage data collection across organizational boundaries and track the completion of collection tasks. 

Our solution allows for the calculation of all categories of corporate emissions, including scope 1, 2, and all sub-categories of scope 3. It supports large-scale calculations based on spending and activities. Additionally, you can leverage an emissions factors library, which includes pre-delivered EPA content. Notably, corporate carbon footprint calculations are a built-in feature of SCT, requiring no additional products or licenses.

With the new Corrective Bookings feature, you can now correct past emissions records within the Emissions DPI. Each correction is fully traceable, displaying the original data alongside the corrected values and the period of correction. This ensures transparency and compliance for auditing purposes.

 

Generate ESG Reports with AI

As of August 2024, you can generate draft reports for periodic internal or external ESG reporting, based on the ESG data available in the SAP Sustainability Control Tower. 

The “Generate ESG Reports with AI” functionality utilizes best-practice templates, generative AI text, and metrics calculated in SAP SCT using the OpenAI GPT 4 model via the SAP AI core service. 

In the first version, users can choose from pre-defined SAP templates. With the planned deliveries in Q4/2024, they will soon be able to edit the templates based on their requirements. Draft reports can be distributed within the organization for reviews, collaboration, and adaptive iterations. 

 

Disclosure Management Strategy

Due to the CSRD disclosure requirements, companies have to also report on how sustainability issues affect their business and the impact of their activities on people and the environment. These requirements are detailed in the European Sustainability Reporting Standards (ESRS), which require a formal disclosure and exchange of disclosure documents via XBRL format with the authorities.

SAP Sustainability Control Tower aggregates, plans, calculates and steers ESG factors and goals.  Across the world, companies strive to improve their environmental, social, and governance (ESG) metrics and disclose them combined with financial data in their integrated reports. SAP SCT can distribute relevant data into existing disclosure management tools (SAP or non-SAP) to generate integrated reporting. 

Our ambition is to share the ESG metrics available in SAP SCT with disclosure management software to enable the creation, validation, and filing or disclosure of regulatory reports in Word, XBRL, and iXBRL formats, including ESRS. 

Therefore, Thomson Reuters will launch an ESG Disclosure Management app (target availability in Q1/2025), which extends SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SAP SCT) to enable customers with a software solution to simplify their ESG reporting and filing needs. This app will integrate SAP SCT with Thomson Reuters' ONESOURCE Statutory Reporting (TR OSR) to provide customers with last-mile reporting functionality based on the SAP SCT data foundation.

Moreover, the upcoming release of SAP Disclosure Management (target availability in Q4/2024) we release SCT data source connection to seamlessly integrate ESG data between the two solutions to save time and ensure ESG data consistency for existing customers.

 

Data Foundation and Extensibility

As we continue to evolve the SAP Sustainability Control Tower, a key focus is enhancing the extensibility of the ESG Data Foundation. This allows organizations to tailor the platform to their unique sustainability reporting needs, ensuring comprehensive and accurate ESG data management.

We are pleased to introduce a new custom metric type: Standard Custom Metrics. This feature allows you to define calculations directly within the Manage Metrics app and perform them in the Manage ESG Data app, using methods such as sum, division, and weighted division.

With this enhancement, we have updated the names of existing custom metrics to better reflect their functionalities:

  • Basic Metric is now Basic Custom Metric: Create and configure your metric in the Manage Metrics app without requiring measures for the calculation. There is no change in functionality.
  • Advanced Metric is now Legacy Advanced Custom Metric: Metrics and calculation methods previously defined in the Manage Metrics app and calculated in CPE. No new metrics of this type can be created.
  • Standard Custom Metric: Newly introduced, allowing you to define your calculation methodology directly in the Manage Metrics app and calculate in the Manage ESG Data app.

These changes enhance the flexibility and precision of your sustainability metrics, enabling more tailored reporting and analysis.

To streamline your data import processes, you can now import data for Basic Custom Metrics directly using CSV files in the Custom Metrics - Actuals tab of the Manage ESG Data app. Templates are readily available within the app to guide you through the process. This update eliminates the need for the CPE environment for this function, ensuring a more efficient workflow without affecting your existing data.

As of October 2024, we’ve expanded the import and export capabilities to include custom metrics alongside custom measures and dimensions. This functionality is available within the Manage Metrics, Manage Measures, and Manage Custom Dimensions apps. You can now export a set of custom objects and import them into another tenant, facilitating easier data transfer and system setup across different environments. For a comprehensive guide on this process, refer to Exporting and Importing Custom Objects.

To facilitate data import processes, you can now use Microsoft Excel (XLSX/XLS) files in addition to CSV files when importing data in the Manage Master Data and Manage ESG Data apps. The system automatically adapts to typical date formats in Excel files, enhancing ease of use and reducing data formatting issues.

To offer greater customization, dimensions with SAP-provided values now have either Read Only or Active status in the Manage Master Data app. You can add new values to dimensions with Active status, allowing you to seamlessly combine SAP-provided and custom values without creating entirely new custom dimensions.

For example, if the "Contract Type" dimension has four default SAP-provided values and you require an additional value, you can simply add it to the existing dimension. This streamlined approach simplifies dimension management and enhances data consistency.

You can import new master data values via file import or use the new Edit function within the Manage Master Data app.

 

Metric Management

Simplified Creation of Standard Custom Metrics

Defining your calculation methods is now more straightforward. With the Manage Metrics app, you can create Standard Custom Metrics and define your calculation methodology directly within the app, choosing from sum, division, and weighted division methods. This eliminates the need for external calculations and streamlines the metric creation process for many additional metrics ESG standards require.

Assurance Information Configuration

You can now configure the disclosure type, applicable reporting standards, set materiality, and specify the assurance level for each metric in the new Assurance section. This enhancement allows for more detailed reporting and better alignment with regulatory requirements.

 

Auditability

In October, the "View Change Logs" application was released, designed to enhance transparency and compliance with regulatory obligations. This feature allows users to track and review changes across various data categories, including metrics, custom measures, dimensions, and activity data for measures. Users can log all key change types—Create, Update, and Delete—along with old and new values, making it easier to monitor data integrity. This functionality is particularly critical for meeting stringent regulatory requirements. For more information, see  Using the View Change Logs Application.

 

SAP Continuous Influence Program

At SAP, customer feedback is at the heart of our innovation process. With the SAP Continuous Influence program, you have the opportunity to directly impact the future development of SAP products and services. By sharing your ideas, needs, and priorities, you can help shape the direction of our solutions and ensure they meet your business needs.

We are excited to announce the launch of our new SAP Influence page for our Sustainability solutions: SFM (Sustainability Footprint Management), SCT (Sustainability Control Tower), and RDP (Responsible Design and Production). These solutions cover key sustainability topics like ESG-related disclosure, Material Transition, and Carbon Management. This dedicated platform allows you, as a customer or partner, to submit feature requests, vote on others' ideas, and receive timely feedback from SAP.

Check out the session at https://influence.sap.com/go/sustainability and start making your voice heard!

 

Planned Feature Updates

We continue to innovate and expand the capabilities of SAP Sustainability Control Tower. Here are some planned features expected in early Q1:

  • We plan to introduce the ability to create qualitative metrics and collect textual information for selected time periods. This includes support for narrative text and simple questions, allowing you to describe actions and policies. You can assign disclosure references and assurance information in the same way as for quantitative metrics, enhancing the depth of your sustainability reporting.
  •  A new application, Analyze ESG Data to be developed, allowing customers and partners to use advanced filtering capabilities for KPI tiles by time-period and hierarchy. You'll be able to cluster and group KPI tiles by subject matter, frameworks, or any user-defined groups, and personalize custom views. This will help you track the performance of your ESG metrics and group them by strategic priorities.
  • We plan to introduce custom authorization policies that allow you to limit user access to transactional data for specified measures and metrics across various applications. This will enhance data security by preventing unauthorized access to sensitive information, such as financial or HR records.
  • The Manage Metrics app will include new classification fields to better describe the qualities of sustainability metrics. This includes Reporting Standard Version, Disclosure Requirement, and Disclosure References. This improvement will enhance data auditability and compliance alignment.

 

Further Assets

  • Product Feature Videos and Roadmap
  • Customer Stories
  • CSRD Whitepaper

 

Disclaimer:

This blog post, or any related document and SAP's strategy and possible future developments, products and or platforms directions and functionality are all subject to change and may be changed by SAP at any time for any reason without notice.

entry.title: Empower your frontline workers with SAP Service and Asset Manager and Samsung Knox Capture
entry.content:
In today’s fast-paced business environment, efficiency and data accuracy are essential. For organizations managing assets, from manufacturing plants to critical infrastructure, mobile enterprise asset management (EAM) solutions have become essential tools as they enable real-time access to asset information and work orders and allow technicians to perform maintenance and repairs more efficiently. 

Integrating scanning functions into these mobile solutions amplifies their impact, streamlining processes with smart scanning and an intuitive data capture. Scanning enables instant, accurate access to data on the go by reducing manual data entry, minimizing errors, and accelerating workflows. What are the main use cases that our mobile team has identified for scanning?  

  1. Rapid Identification and Tracking of Assets 
    Scanning features allow technicians to instantly access an asset’s data by simply scanning a barcode or QR code. This immediate access can include assets, spare parts, maintenance work orders and notifications. For organizations with large inventories, scanning technology eliminates the need for manual searches and ensures quick, accurate identification of stock. 
  2. Improved Data Accuracy 
    Manual data entry is both time-consuming and prone to error. Scanning eliminates the risk of transcription errors, helping to keep data records accurate ultimately minimizing administrative overhead and ensuring the reliability of asset and inventory data.

SAP Service and Asset Manager and Samsung Knox Capture  

As announced in one of our previous blog posts (Navigating the speed bumps across the supply chain) that introduced SAP and Samsung partnership motivation and areas of collaboration, we are integrating our mobile flagship application SAP Service and Asset Manager with Samsung Knox Capture as part of the 2410 release.  

This integration will help enable the user to scan barcodes and automate filling of input fields during maintenance and inventory activities. The programmable Hardware Key on Samsung Galaxy rugged devices will also enable 1 click trigger of scanning functionality, allowing users to optimize their daily activity by enabling them to scan single or multi barcodes simultaneously based on their use case needs with the simple press of a hardware key.  Details here: SAP Service and Asset Manager and Samsung Knox capture integration 

Why Samsung Knox for an integrated data capture? 

  • Smart scanning: Faster camera boot-up, accurate auto-focus and tight Samsung hardware integration 
  • Intuitive data capture: Single, selective, or all-at-once batch scanning from integrated in SAP Service and Asset Manager maintenance and inventory mobile workflows. Hardware key to trigger scanning, or keyboard wedge 
  • Effortless deployment: Tailored for SAP Service and Asset Manager SDK and provided as part of the out of the box feature set when customer choses Samsung devices. 

Conclusion 

The integration of scanning functionalities into mobile enterprise asset management apps represents a significant advancement in technology. By improving accuracy, efficiency, and compliance, scanning tools help companies streamline their operations, reduce costs, and empower their workforce. SAP Service and Asset Manager and Samsung Knox integration helps organizations with business ready capabilities that combines the software and hardware strengths of SAP and Samsung. 

#SAPServiceandAssetManager #SamsungKnoxCapture

entry.title: 2024 SAP BTP Year in Review: Release Highlights for SAP S/4HANA users
entry.content:
2024 was an action-packed year in SAP Business Technology Platform solutions, with great technology updates to help you think ahead, adapt and keep things running smoothly.  This article highlights some of the new features released in SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) solutions between January 2024 and November 2024 that are most relevant for SAP S/4HANA users.

As a quick recap, SAP Business Technology Platform brings together application development, automation, integration, data and analytics, and AI capabilities into one unified environment optimized for SAP applications. SAP BTP is designed to complement both SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition and SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition.  There are more than 80 different services in SAP BTP, in this article I'll focus on SAP Integration Suite, SAP Business AI, SAP Build, SAP Analytics Cloud, SAP DataSphere, and SAP HANA Cloud.

So, what’s new? Let’s explore them now!

SAP Integration Suite: Supporting a wider range of integration needs

As a critical part of SAP BTP, SAP Integration Suite has been a reliable source of integration technology for SAP S/4HANA Cloud. And the introduction of new features is making these connections more streamlined, situation aware, and productive—no matter how complex the landscape.

  • Event-driven architecture
    SAP Integration Suite now supports event-driven architecture to enable real-time processes that scale efficiently across distributed environments. It includes advanced event mesh capabilities that allow organizations to handle peak loads with greater ease and run more responsive and resilient operations.

  • Expanded integration library
    The curated library of integrations now includes ServiceNow and Workday solutions, expanding our portfolio to more than 3,000 connectors. Built and maintained by SAP and our partners, these integrations simplify connectivity to third-party applications—enabling smoother workflows and faster deployment.

  • On-premise solution workload management
    Extending beyond cloud-based connections, SAP Integration Suite supports ground-to-ground processes so companies can connect on-premise systems directly. This functionality provides greater flexibility for organizations with hybrid or fully on-premise environments.

  • API fabric for centralized access
    The API fabric enhances developer productivity by centralizing SAP and third-party APIs in a unified catalog. Developers can now quickly discover, register, and access a broad array of APIs, simplifying integration development and accelerating project timelines.

  • EOIO processing
    To support complex asynchronous integration scenarios, SAP Integration Suite now includes “exactly once in order” (EOIO) processing. This capability helps ensure messages are delivered exactly once and in the correct sequence while reducing data errors and enhancing consistency.

🔖Learn more about SAP Integration Suite's new features.

SAP Business AI: Making autonomous finance a reality

 The introduction of autonomous finance capabilities in SAP BTP is making a finance function that requires minimal human intervention a practical and productive reality. Enabled by the addition of SAP Business AI, this update automates complex financial operations—helping companies run with improved agility and resilience.

Leveraging the advanced AI and machine learning models of SAP Business AI, the platform provides SAP S/4HANA Cloud with tools that automate routine tasks, such as transaction processing and reconciliations. ​Recent benchmarking of SAP customers shows that adopting these capabilities paves the way to 90% more-efficient financial reporting, 7% higher forecast accuracy, and 10 times faster planning cycles.

In addition, the enhanced predictive capabilities and real-time data analytics available in SAP BTP equip SAP S/4HANA Cloud with insights that go beyond traditional finance automation. Organizations can now manage risk, increase transparency, and improve accuracy as the cloud ERP anticipates financial outcomes and adapts proactively to changes in cash flow or market volatility.

🔖Learn more about autonomous finance

SAP Build: Stepping up development with ABAP and generative AI

SAP Build solutions have become critical tools for the fast development of applications and automations with a simplified and streamlined builder interface.  Announced in October 2024, The latest integration of ABAP development tools and generative AI in SAP Build is making that experience even better for developers of all skill levels looking to modernize and extend their ERP capabilities.

By connecting tools such as ABAP, Java, JavaScript, and low-code in a unified environment, SAP Build enables direct development access within SAP S/4HANA Cloud, making customization faster and easier. The extensibility wizard allows extensions for fields, logic, and processes while maintaining core system integrity.

Prebuilt content in SAP Build accelerates work across functions, including HR, spend management, and procurement. For example, the Joule copilot boosts efficiency with code completion, workflow automation, and instant help access. Developers can also create custom AI skills with drag-and-drop simplicity.

🔖Learn more the latest in SAP Build for SAP S/4HANA

SAP Datasphere: Making data handling simpler and more reliable

SAP Datasphere focused on improving access to mission-critical business data across any cloud infrastructure with a business data fabric.  In July 2024, the following updates gave business users stronger control over data views, resources, and import flexibility.

  • Compatibility contracts
    By setting a data view’s release state to “Released,” business users can help ensure output columns remain consistent until they’re replaced by a successor. This feature also enables gradual decommissioning while maintaining system integrity.

  • Workload management
    Resource management can be centralized by defining priorities and statement limits for all spaces. In return, business users can manage workload distribution effectively, while deprecating parameters for the command-line interface simplify updates in future releases.

  • Space changes during content import
    Specific spaces can now be chosen during imports. Space mapping added flexibility to support intra-tenant transport. Business users can import content into different spaces within the same tenant, even creating multiple copies in various spaces if needed.

🔖Learn more about SAP Datasphere's new features

SAP Analytics Cloud: Improving insight depth and speed

SAP Analytics Cloud, also part of SAP BTP, has been enhanced with several powerful updates that streamline data analysis, planning, and presentation. With these new features, SAP Analytics Cloud is adding even more depth and flexibility for customers using SAP S/4HANA Cloud, making it easier for teams across the business to access insights and make data-driven decisions.

Here’s a look at some of the most impactful advancements:

  • Report-report interface
    Organizations with live connections to the SAP Business Warehouse (SAP BW) application can now leverage the report-report interface to analyze data in context. With this feature, users can jump from making queries in SAP BW—from generating ABAP reports and transactions to analyzing data.

  • Natural language queries
    Enabled by generative AI, the just ask feature of SAP Analytics Cloud allows users to ask questions in their natural language and receive instant data-driven answers in SAP S/4HANA Cloud while reducing their dependence on IT support. Recent updates include tools to simplify the user experience, such as the prompt guide, model control options for administrators, and advanced comparisons.

  • Plan-data files uploading
    Starting with the Q3 2024 release, business users are now able to enhance their planning efficiency by uploading their plan-data files from SAP S/4HANA Cloud directly into SAP Analytics Cloud. They can initiate uploads through triggers in stories, and administrators can set up data structures and upload jobs to help ensure data consistency.

  • Standard unit conversion
    The standard unit conversion feature simplifies data handling by allowing modelers and planners to use predefined conversion tables for measurements like length and weight. This capability helps ensure consistent unit representation across reports and stories, supporting data accuracy in SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

SAP Analytics Cloud (part 2): Extending planning and analysis business-wide

This year’s updates also brought powerful enhancements for planning and analysis in SAP Analytics Cloud. The following innovations were added to SAP Analytics Cloud to further streamline workflows, foster collaboration, and support strategic insights across departments, particulary for users with SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

  • Triggering of calendar events from external applications
    Enhancing workflow automation for enterprise planning scenarios, this capability simplifies end-to-end planning processes and helps ensure key tasks are executed without disruption across integrated platforms.

  • Automated synchronization of calendar events
    With automatic synchronization of SAP calendar events with Microsoft Teams and Outlook, users can keep track of critical planning deadlines and events. This integration improves productivity and collaboration, embedding SAP planning directly into users’ daily calendars.

  • Industry-specific business content
    New and updated content packages in SAP Analytics Cloud provide prebuilt templates and analytics tools for business areas, such as sustainability, finance, sales, and the public sector. These packages accelerate the implementation of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, allowing companies to leverage industry-specific insights more quickly and effectively.

  • Probability simulation
    The compass functionality of SAP Analytics Cloud facilitates probability simulations that allow organizations to anticipate risks and model scenarios across key business drivers. Decision-makers can simulate the best, worst, and realistic outcomes—leading to more-proactive strategies to mitigate emerging disruptions.

  • Continuous planning integration
    The integration feature for SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere forms a unified, governed data environment for planning and analytics. By centralizing data on one platform, this feature fosters collaboration across departments and reduces time-to-action for mission-critical decisions.

🔖Learn more about the new features in SAP Analytics Cloud

SAP HANA Cloud: Extending AI support and larger data volumes

 

SAP HANA Cloud is SAP’s database as a service solution, designed for large scale deployments.  The new features in SAP HANA Cloud for 2024 provide improved support for artificial intelligence and larger deployements, along with many quality of life features. Here are just a few of the highlights:

  • General availability of the Vector Engine for SAP HANA Clou
    The introduction of the vector engine allows SAP HANA Cloud to provide the foundation for large language models, facilitating more contextually accurate responses to business queries. Learn more in this related blog post.

  • Support for larger instances
    With the 2024 Q2 release, the amount of memory available to allocate to a database instance increased to 12 TB. This allows better support for memory-intensive workloads.
  • Cloud Reliability – Backup Compression
    New backup compression for data, log, and catalog backups for SAP HANA database instances in SAP HANA Cloud reduces the amount of backup storage consumed, while ensuring the integrity and security of the backups.

  • Online migration
    The newly introduced online migration capabilities allow critical business processes to remain operational within the source SAP HANA system during the migration process. This innovation minimizes migration downtime, automates migration tasks, and reduces the need for manual interventions.

  • New machine learning options
    Multiple new features to the Predictive Analysis Library (PAL) in SAP HANA Cloud facilitates faster modeling and application of AutoML, and automate analytics processes. The features allow a single neural network model to predict multiple related target columns. Other enhancements include the hierarchical density-based clustering of data with noise (HDBSCAN) and support for clustering of vector data using the DBSCAN and HDBSCAN functions.
    Learn more in the blog post provided by Christoph Morgen, as well as in this community post
  • Support for AWS PrivateLink services
    This new capability enables users to connect applications running within an Amazon Web Services (AWS) to SAP HANA Cloud using private IP endpoints, enhancing security. Learn more in this demo video.

Many more new features are described by @thomashammer in his blog post series.  

🔖 Learn more about SAP HANA Cloud

Closing

These new features in SAP BTP don't just make it easier to get the most from the data and processes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud—they also build a solid foundation for growth and adaptability. When you use these tools together, you’re creating an ERP experience that’s ready for whatever comes next.

Learn more about how SAP BTP enhances SAP S/4HANA Cloud at sap.com/btp-for-s4hana.

 

Explore more related content in this series about SAP BTP with S/4HANA:

Data Migration

  • SAP S/4HANA Data Migration and Master Data Management Best Practices with SAP BTP

Extensions

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Extensions with SAP Build Best Practices: An Expert Roundtable
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Extensions and Clean Core with SAP BTP - A Best Practices Conversation

Artificial Intelligence

  • Artificial Intelligence (AI) for SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP: A deep dive
  • How SAP Business AI and Large Language Models are shaping the future of SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP

entry.title: SAP BTP ABAP Environment - Release 2411
entry.content:
SAP BTP ABAP Environment - Release 2411

As follow-up to our previous release 2408, this blog post provides an update concerning our most recent release 2411, which is available for customers and partners since November 16th. This particular release features highlights such as ABAP Cloud in SAP Build, a new approach to build Application Jobs, and the region-less subscription of the Landscape Portal.

The complete list of all new features can be found on the What´s New for SAP BTP ABAP Environment page.

ABAP Development Tools (ADT)

  • Creating an ABAP Cloud Project in the Context of the SAP BTP ABAP environment, a service key is no longer needed when creating an ABAP Cloud Project. You now enter the ABAP Service instance URL (details)
  • Triggering Where-Used List for Usages of CDS Annotation to enable the investigation in which ABAP CDS an annotation is used
  • Displaying Field Characteristics for RAP Types in the Element Information Popup, The Element Information popup (F2) for RAP types shows additional field characteristics information such as mandatory:create, mandatory:execute or readonly (details)
  • Multi-Dimensional Analysis App Generator, you can now use the multi-dimensional analysis app generator wizard to create all ABAP development objects that are needed to build, expose, and deploy a multi-dimensional analysis Fiori app (details)
  • Support of SAP Fiori launchpad app descriptor items in ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse, these items describe how an application is started and consist of a target mapping and one or more tiles, for more information see SAP Fiori Applications in the ABAP Environment and Working with Launchpad App Descriptor Items
  • Rerunning Unit Tests with Coverage in the ABAP Coverage view (details)
  • Clearing Coverage results, you can now clear your coverage results in the ABAP Coverage view (details)

ABAP Test Cockpit (ATC)

  • ATC Check "Usage of Released APIs" can be run for ABAP Cloud Objects

Reuse Services

  • New Class Interface for Application Job Catalog Entries and Templates, with the new attribute-based class interface it is now possible to change the parameters, sections, and groups of a catalog entry (details)
  • Business Rule Framework (BRF plus) is enhanced with a code generation service and an API introduced to facilitate the consumption of business rules within ABAP Cloud (details)
  • XCO XSLX: You can now style the cells of an Excel worksheet. You can also modify existing documents (details)
  • XCO CDS: You can now generate CDS external entities and logical external schemas, and you can now add comments and arithmetic expressions to CDS views (details)

UI Services and SAP Fiori Launchpad

  • Creating Launchpad App Descriptor Items (details)

Integration

  • With Business Event Logging, you can capture and log business events locally within the ABAP environment
  • App Display Business Event Logs. It provides an overview of logged business events for different objects and event types. You can explore the details of the event with this app
  • App Display Business Events by Objects: It groups business events by instances of objects and object components
  • App Display Changes to Objects: You can see the changes for your objects on a field level as recorded in business event logging

Security

  • ABAP environment tenants are now equipped with a signed default client certificate, which is automatically rotated and can be used for customer managed integration scenarios
  • The communication scenario SAP_COM_0915 provides access to APIs to read the Read Access Logs from the system, the API can for instance be leveraged by a Security Incident and Event Management (SIEM) system
  • TLS 1.3, we now support Transport Layer Security 1.3 for outbound HTTP, WebSocket RFC, and SMTP protocols
  • The system exposes APIs to retrieve customer-managed security settings in a read-only manner

Custom Code Migration App

  • Include ADT links in SAP S/4HANA Readiness Check Export, the export of ATC results for the SAP S/4HANA Readiness Check now includes HTTP(S) links, which enables you to navigate to the source code of example code in ADT for Eclipse. This new functionality is available in the Custom Code Migration app only (details)
  • New option to start analysis after project creation, you can now choose whether you would like to start the custom code analysis run automatically at project creation or whether you would like to schedule it manually after project creation (details)
  • Changes in Scope Calculation Automation, you now need to initiate scope calculation manually after project creation, the scope calculation no longer starts automatically at project creation (details)

Cloud ATC

  • Change Validity Date for ATC Exemptions in the Approve ATC Exemptions App (details)
  • Replication of ATC Exemptions from on-premise towards SAP BTP, Customers need to use transaction SATC_EXPORT_XMPT to download the exemptions from an on-premise ATC system (details)

Technical Monitoring

  • Enhanced app HANA Table Analysis: With the new Column Loads and Column Unloads screens in the HANA Table Analysis app, you can check how many columns of the tables in your HANA database have been loaded to or unloaded from the main storage (details)
  • New app HANA Memory Analysis: With the new app, you can get an overview of the memory consumption of the HANA memory components over time. This helps you to identify bottlenecks in your HANA memory (details)

Lifecycle Management

  • When cloning a software component, you can now optionally use the newly added parameters to request cloning a Bring Your Own Git (BYOG) repository (details)

abapGit

  • Support of object deletions, it can now be exported from and imported to the ABAP environment with abapGit (details)

Landscape Portal

  • Extended Access, you can now access and subscribe to the Landscape Portal for SAP BTP ABAP Environment from every region that is available for the ABAP environment. In regions without SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery service, some applications in the Landscape Portal are limited or not available (details)
  • Deinstalling Products, the new Undeploy Product app in the Landscape Portal enables you to permanently deinstall existing products (details)
